summary: Comprehensive testing completed for Dams Car Rental iteration 5 (admin image upload feature). All 54 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified and working correctly. New features: Admin can upload car images via expo-image-picker (Gallery/Camera buttons) or paste URL, images stored in /backend/uploads/, static file serving at /api/uploads/{filename}, car list shows 56x42 thumbnails, image preview with remove button. Fixed bug: image URL was returning internal cluster URL instead of public URL (now uses X-Forwarded-Host header). No critical issues found.
seed_data_creation: No additional seed data created. App uses existing 6 cars across 4 cities and 8 locations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 features all working. New: Admin image upload for cars. Backend: POST /api/upload/image (admin only) accepts base64 image_data, stores to /backend/uploads/, returns public URL using X-Forwarded-Host header. GET /api/uploads/{filename} serves static files (public access). Supports jpg, jpeg, png, webp extensions. Frontend: admin.tsx updated with expo-image-picker integration - Gallery button (pickImage) and Camera button (takePhoto, native only). Image preview with remove button (X). URL paste input as fallback. Car list shows 56x42 thumbnails. app.json has camera/photo permissions. ImageUpload Pydantic model (image_data, filename). All previous features still working (auth, cars, bookings, admin panel, locations, reviews).

image_url_internal_cluster.issue: Image URL was returning internal cluster URL (http://rental-routes.cluster-0.preview.emergentcf.cloud/api/uploads/...) instead of public URL (https://rental-routes.preview.emergentagent.com/api/uploads/...)
image_url_internal_cluster.root_cause: request.base_url in FastAPI returns internal service URL in Kubernetes environment
image_url_internal_cluster.fix: Updated server.py line 788-799 to use X-Forwarded-Host header from ingress to construct public URL. Falls back to FRONTEND_URL env var if available, then X-Forwarded-Host, then request.base_url
image_url_internal_cluster.verification: Tested with curl and pytest - image URLs now return correct public domain and are accessible
